zh.lifeSummary: Andrea Aldonza Diaz de Mena於1196年出生在西班牙。她至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Andrea Aldonza Diaz de Mena于1196年出生在西班牙。她至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Diaz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Rafael, Miguel, Pedro, Roberto.
Spanish (Díaz): patronymic from the medieval personal name Didacus (see Diego ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Diazdeleon
meanings: Spanish: combination of the surname Díaz (see Diaz ) and the name León (see Leon ) with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Diego
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Francisco, Juan, Fernando, Jose, Miguel, Mario, Maximo, Salvador, Aida, Ana, Domingo.

Spanish: from the very common male personal name Diego, which in the Spanish-speaking world is usually taken to be a shortened form of Santiago . However, this may be only a folk etymology. The personal name is found in the Middle Ages in the Latin forms Didacus and Didagus, probably a derivative of Greek didachē ‘doctrine, teaching’. However, in view of the fact that this personal name is unknown outside the Iberian Peninsula, it may actually have a pre-Roman origin.

variants.name: Diez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Juan, Fernando, Pedro, Rafael, Roberto, Angel, Eduardo, Mario, Ricardo.
Spanish (Díez): variant of Díaz (see Diaz ).
German: variant of   Dietz  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mena
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Juan, Manuel, Luis, Pedro, Ricardo, Alberto, Javier, Jorge, Rafael, Jesus.  Portuguese Marcio, Paulo.  Italian Antonio, Marco, Angelo, Caesar, Didio, Luciano, Annalisa, Bartolo, Cecilio, Clemente, Clementina, Dario.
Spanish: habitational name from Mena, a place in Castile and León provinces.
Italian: from the female personal name Mena, a short form of Filomena (see Filomeno ) or Domenica (see Domenico ).

variants.name: Filomeno
meanings: Italian, Portuguese, and Catalan: from the personal name Filomeno, from Greek Philomenēs, a name apparently composed of the elements philein ‘to love’ and menos ‘strength’. This surname is very rare in Spain (it is found only in Catalonia); as a name of Catalan or Spanish origin it is most common in Mexico and the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Andrea
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of disputed origin. It has been in use since the 17th century. It is now generally taken as a feminine equivalent of Andreas , and this probably represents its actual origin. However, it was not in use in the Middle Ages, and the suggestion has also been made that it represents a coinage in English from the Greek vocabulary word andreia ‘manliness, virility’.

variants.name: Andrés
meanings: From New Testament Greek. See Andrew .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Andrea Aldonza Diaz de Mena was born in 1196, in Spain. She had at least 1 daughter.
